description: A column or table was dropped in the same release that stopped writing it, breaking old code or rollback.
body: Failure mode: deploy order or rollback runs old code against a schema where the old column no longer exists, or data needed for recovery has already been deleted. Diagnostic trigger: migration combines code switch, backfill, read change, and destructive cleanup in one step. Do-not-use signal: do not approve destructive cleanup without evidence that no active code path reads or writes the old shape. Recovery: split into expand, compatible code, backfill, read switch, verification, and later contract cleanup; keep source data until verification passes. Risk boundary: irreversible drops require backup or explicit roll-forward plan and human approval for production-sized data.
